title: Optional REST parameters should have an object type
description: When defining optional parameters in REST APIs, using object types instead of scalar types (such as int, boolean, etc.) is recommended. This ensures that the absence of a value can be properly represented as null, avoiding ambiguity between a default value and an unset parameter.
recommendation: Use object types (e.g., Integer, Boolean) for optional REST parameters instead of primitive types.
impact: Using scalar types for optional REST parameters can lead to confusion and bugs, as it is not possible to distinguish between a default value and a missing value.

checks.message: Use object types (e.g., Integer, Boolean) for optional REST parameters instead of primitive types.
